description: SIL Multi-Dictionary Formatter (MDF) marker profile for the CDSL third export view. Standard-format markers (Toolbox/FLEx lineage) over the neutral model, MW-focused (national language = English). Implements docs/MDF_EXPORT_MAPPING.md; every lossy adequacy call is surfaced as a \nt model-loss marker.
fieldOrderNote: The canonical relative field order per Coward & Grimes 2000 App. B ('Relative order of fields in an entry'), filtered to this profile's marker inventory: identity (\lx \hm \lc), \se, grammar (\ps), sense block (\sn \ge \re \de \xv \xn — \re sits between \ge and \de in App. B, a reversal pointer keyed off the gloss/definition pair it reverses), typed relations (\lf \le, App. D) before the untyped cross-reference (\cf), \va, etymology (\et \es), \sd, then \bb, then \nt last. Notably \cf/\lf precede \et/\es in App. B, not after as a naive alphabetic-ish guess would place them — verified against the book, not assumed. export-mdf.mjs emits fields in this relative order within a record; validate-mdf-profile.mjs checks it. Fields this profile never emits for MW (\se, \re, \de, \xv, \xn, \va, \sd) keep their reserved slot for when the profile extends past MW.

markerRoles.\\lc: citation / segmented form (MW <k2> when it carries a morpheme boundary)
markerRoles.\\se: subentry (reserved; not emitted — MDF's subentry groups sub-senses under a parent headword, not a semantic decomposition, per App. B)
markerRoles.\\ps: part of speech, with gender folded in (MW <lex> or derived grammar)
markerRoles.\\sn: sense number (inferred from prose segmentation, not observed)
markerRoles.\\ge: English gloss — the *interlinear/secondary* gloss language, App. B: 'supplanted by a \de' (i.e. \ge and \de are mutually exclusive alternates at the same slot, never both for one sense). MW's national language is English, so MW glosses go here.
markerRoles.\\re: reversal-English (App. B: sits directly after \ge, before \de/\we) — a curated headword-form pointer for a finderlist/index, built only from curated \ge/\de fields, never mined from body text (RESOLVED per the SIL correlation doc: csl-atlas body/reverse headword-mining was rejected at 38.6% precision; reserved, not emitted by any current profile).
markerRoles.\\de: national/target-language definition — the *primary* prose definition, App. B's alternate to \ge at the same slot. Reserved for MW (English is already \ge there); ruled the target field for Sanskrit-Sanskrit koshas (SKD/VCP) once their MDF profile is built, since Sanskrit is their national language, not a secondary gloss.
markerRoles.\\xv: example sentence (empty for MW: a retrieval dictionary with no examples)
markerRoles.\\lf: lexical function (typed relation) — App. D's controlled vocabulary (Syn, Ant, Compound, Part, Whole, Gen, Spec, …). Emitted for compound decomposition (\lf Compound, App. D: 'Lexicalized compound using headword not easily handled by other lexical functions' — a direct fit). NOT emitted for q.v. cross-references: no App. D function cleanly types a generic 'see also' pointer, so those stay plain \cf rather than guess a type.
markerRoles.\\le: lexical function value, English (App. B: 'combines with \lf') — paired with each \lf line, carries the related headword.
markerRoles.\\cf: cross-reference (q.v. targets; MDF's own 'See:' field — already the correct home for an untyped see-also pointer, per App. B)
markerRoles.\\va: variant form (reserved; not emitted by the MW-only first pass)
markerRoles.\\bb: bibliography / source citation (MW <ls>; the L. hedge is kept as \bb L.)
markerRoles.\\nt: note; model-loss markers use the 'model-loss:' prefix, provenance the 'meta:' prefix

notePrefixes.meta:: per-record provenance: profile version, scope, review status, entry type, MW source pointer
notePrefixes.model-loss:: a lossy adequacy call made visible (hedge, root, continuation)
